title: Batch All Triplet Loss
description:
Implement Batch All triplet mining: compute the loss over ALL valid (anchor, positive, negative) triplets within a batch, averaging only over triplets with positive loss (hard triplets).

A valid triplet (i, j, k) requires:
- `labels[i] == labels[j]` (i and j are same class)
- `labels[i] != labels[k]` (k is different class)
- `i != j`

`L = mean over hard triplets of max(0, D(a,p) - D(a,n) + margin)`

A triplet is 'hard' if its loss > 0.
constraints: Average only over hard (positive-loss) triplets; if none return 0
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def batch_all_triplet_loss(embeddings: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray, margin: float = 0.2) -> float:
    """Batch All triplet loss.

    Args:
        embeddings: shape (B, D)
        labels: class labels, shape (B,)
        margin: triplet margin

    Returns:
        Mean loss over hard triplets
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def batch_all_triplet_loss(embeddings: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray, margin: float = 0.2) -> float:
    embeddings = np.asarray(embeddings, dtype=float)
    labels = np.asarray(labels)
    diff = embeddings[:, None, :] - embeddings[None, :, :]  # (B, B, D)
    dist = np.sqrt(np.sum(diff ** 2, axis=-1) + 1e-12)     # (B, B)
    B = len(labels)
    # Build masks
    pos_mask = (labels[:, None] == labels[None, :])  # (B, B) same class
    neg_mask = ~pos_mask                              # different class
    eye = np.eye(B, dtype=bool)
    pos_mask &= ~eye  # exclude self
    # Triplet loss: (B, B, B) but computed efficiently
    # L[i,j,k] = max(0, dist[i,j] - dist[i,k] + margin) for valid (i,j,k)
    d_ap = dist[:, :, None]   # (B, B, 1) - anchor-positive dist
    d_an = dist[:, None, :]   # (B, 1, B) - anchor-negative dist
    raw = np.maximum(0.0, d_ap - d_an + margin)  # (B, B, B)
    # Valid mask: j is positive for i, k is negative for i
    valid = pos_mask[:, :, None] & neg_mask[:, None, :]  # (B, B, B)
    hard_losses = raw[valid & (raw > 1e-16)]
    return float(np.mean(hard_losses)) if len(hard_losses) > 0 else 0.0
explanation: Batch All uses all valid triplets, giving more gradient signal per batch than hard mining alone. Averaging only over hard triplets (loss > 0) prevents the mean from being diluted by easy triplets. The trade-off: Batch All can be noisy early in training when many triplets are hard; Batch Hard is more stable.

test_cases.name: Perfectly separated loss=0
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
emb = np.array([[0.0,0],[0.1,0],[10.0,0],[10.1,0]])
labels = np.array([0,0,1,1])
assert batch_all_triplet_loss(emb, labels, margin=0.2) == 0.0

test_cases.name: Overlapping classes has loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
emb = np.array([[0.0,0],[1.0,0],[0.5,0],[1.5,0]])
labels = np.array([0,1,0,1])
loss = batch_all_triplet_loss(emb, labels, margin=0.2)
assert loss >= 0

test_cases.name: Non-negative result
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(2)
emb = np.random.randn(6, 4)
labels = np.array([0,0,1,1,2,2])
assert batch_all_triplet_loss(emb, labels) >= 0

test_cases.name: Single class returns 0
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
emb = np.random.randn(4, 3)
labels = np.array([0,0,0,0])
assert batch_all_triplet_loss(emb, labels) == 0.0

hints: Build full (B,B,B) triplet loss tensor using broadcasting
